76135.subject: 🟪 Friday Charts
76135.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9f199753-ca31-4e3d-9435-5e12896ecff5/BD_NL_Banner_Heading.png?t=1781793903)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e60d7cde-4250-41f8-9443-28ed51b80560/divider_transparent.png?t=1774649182)
Caption: 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3bd8a492-ff61-41db-a7f2-c821d481fd72/BD_NL_QuoteCircle.png?t=1781794162)
Caption: 

==“Someone said to me 'To you football is a matter of life or death!' and I said 'Listen, it's more important than that.'”==
_— _==_Bill Shankly_==



----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3c0c80ec-ec26-4f80-8935-06ac36a0ff2d/image.png?t=1784320312)
Caption: 

# ==Friday Charts==

France played most of the first match of the first World Cup with ten men after their goalkeeper suffered a broken jaw contesting a shot. There were no substitutions in 1930, so they put a midfielder in goal.

They won anyway, 4-1 over Mexico, but hardly anyone saw it. Attendance was estimated at just 4,400 spectators. Only 300 watched Romania beat Peru.

The entire tournament was played in one city, Montevideo, Uruguay.

The match balls were not always the same. Argentina and Uruguay both insisted on using their own. When they met in the final, Argentina’s ball was used in the first half and Uruguay’s in the second. 

Wardrobes were not always coordinated. When Bolivia played Brazil, both teams had brought white jerseys to play in. Bolivia borrowed Uruguay's blue shirts for the match. 

For their first match, Bolivia had played in jerseys that spelled out Viva Uruguay. The third “U” was unfortunately missing for the pre-game photo shoot:

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e806c0c4-6522-4505-82ea-07e782e7a859/image.png?t=1784320465)
Caption: 

Why _Bolivia_ wore Uruguay on their shirts is sadly lost to history, but it might have been because the Uruguayan Football Association had covered some of their expenses.

There was no prize money on offer. To attract teams, Uruguay offered to pay for everyone’s travel and accommodation.

Even so, the cost was part of the reason why only four of the 13 participating teams came from Europe. England, Italy, Spain, Germany and Holland all decided to stay home. 

In 1930, it took 16 days to cross the Atlantic by steamship, so European players would be away from home for roughly two months — which meant being away from their day jobs for two months.

Most football players were amateurs at the time, but even the professionals would have needed a day job to supplement th

76134.subject: Onchain Assets Weekly: 7/17/26
76134.body:
Onchain Assets Weekly

Автор: Securitize


Автор: Securitize

This week’s highlights: Cantor Fitzgerald partners with Securitize; Securitize expands board; Carlos Domingo on The Daily Wolf with Scott Melker; Securitize Fund Services Joins BACI; and Benchmark reaffirms Securitize rating. Cantor Fitzgerald Eyes Blockchain-based IPO Shares in New Tie-up with Securitize | Fortune Securitize and Cantor Fitzgerald are bringing IPOs and follow-on offerings onchain, creating a regulated way for companies to raise capital and conduct IPOs using blockchain-based infrastructure. READ MORE Securitize Appoints Rebecca Macieira-Kaufmann and Manolo Sánchez to Board | citybiz Securitize appointed Rebecca Macieira-Kaufmann and Manolo Sánchez to its board, adding veteran financial services leadership as it expands regulated infrastructure for digital securities and onchain capital markets. READ MORE Securitize CEO Explains Onchain vs. Traditional Investing | Yahoo Finance Scott Melker sits down with the Founder and CEO of Carlos Domingo to discuss the key differences between buying Securitize onchain versus through a traditional brokerage account. WATCH NOW Securitize Fund Services Joins BACI Member | Blockchain Association of the Cayman Islands Securitize Fund Services brings tokenization-ready fund administration to Blockchain Association of the Cayman Islands (BACI), supporting 650+ funds across private markets, digital assets, and onchain workflows. READ MORE Benchmark Says Securitize Investors Should 'Strip Out the Noise' After Post-SPAC Selloff | The Block Benchmark reiterated its “buy” rating and $16 price target on SECZ as Securitize announced a partnership with Cantor Fitzgerald to support onchain IPOs and secondary offerings. READ MORE

76133.subject: The Daily Degen - July 17th, 2026
76133.body:
View this post on the web at https://thedailydegen.substack.com/p/the-daily-degen-july-17th-2026

Happy Friday good frogs!
Let’s get after it!
As always… stats/alerts/etc at top and tweets/news/links/videos/etc at bottom… and all tweets are hyperlinked so just click on them to pull them up on Twitter/X!
Market Update
Crypto (from @CryptoBubbles
Total Crypto Market Cap: $2.228T
QQQ (Nasdaq 100): $697.15
Gold: $4,009.80
Silver: $56.02
Oil (WTI): $81.92
US 10Y Treasury: 4.549%
Crypto Fear & Greed Index (from coinmarketcap(dot)com): 33
Biggest Price Movers
(From @coingecko top 1000, by 24 hour change) 
Spotlight
Quai continues to see huge success as 2026 rolls on!
Quai defi is making headlines as of late, and Quainance is now live on the network, facilitating some massive defi-maxxing opportunities [ https://substack.com/redirect/55d4efdf-1831-457e-bbfe-ee2651b4b990?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] for Quai-enthusiasts! (see below!) 
In addition, there was an extremely cool recent announcement as well, concerning the team’s paper- “Optimal Reward Allocation via Proportional Splitting”- that has been accepted to the Advances in Financial Technologies (AFT) 2026 conference at the London School of Economics in London!
The paper documents the novel architecture that allows PoW to scale- based on something the Quai gigabrains call’ workshares’- and they will be presenting it later this year at the above-noted conference (sponsored by a16z and Input/Out).  
Once again, with R&D out of the University of Texas, @QuaiNetwork [ https://substack.com/redirect/b6b08974-b677-4265-a974-3ddfe2d9f6f6?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] is a highly scalable proof-of-work EVM-compatible blockchain based on tokenizing energy, and incorporating a gigabrain dual-token model involving $QUAI and $QI - with $QUAI as the main token and $QI as a privacy flatcoin that also is the world’s first “energy dollar”… 
We are always pumped to be spreading the good word about what Quai is up to, so make sure to follow @QuaiNetwork [ https://substack.com/redirect/b9d1dad7-2ccf-40df-9c9b-c10e4ecf20fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] on Twitter/X for more info!
Biggest TVL Movers + Other Interesting Data
Chains are mixed today. Fastest-growing chains on the weekly with at least $10M TVL include Robinhood Chain, XDC, and Monad.  
Protocols are also mixed today. Fastest-growing protocols on the weekly with at least $10M TVL include Arcus (uncategorized protocol on Robinhood Chain), Twyne (lending protocol on ETH Mainnet), and Baseline Protocol (multichain liquidity manager).

Here’s The Top 10 Entities By 7 Day Fee Generation (from @DefiLlama):

Bringing global finance onchain



 
👻 Editor's note: We launched Aave Daily, a new Telegram channel where we'll share the latest Aave, GHO, and governance news each
day. Join here [https://t.me/aavedaily].
 
Highlights
 
Introducing Stable Vaults
Aave Labs announced Stable Vaults, an all-in-one solution for embedding fixed-rate stablecoin yield into any financial product.
They convert variable onchain rates into a fixed rate, so fintechs can offer a one-tap earn feature without building
infrastructure from scratch. Stable Vaults already power the Aave App, and now any business can build on them. Learn more here
[https://aave.com/stable-vaults].
 
Aave V4 Launches on Avalanche
Aave V4 launched [https://x.com/aave/status/2077414474017829232] on Avalanche, its first multi-chain expansion, on July 15 with
one Core Liquidity Hub and three markets. Incentives went live the following day [https://x.com/aave/status/2077757547633639446]
for supplying USDC or USDT and for borrowing AVAX.
 
Aave V3 on Monad Approaches $400M Deposits
Aave V3 on Monad is approaching $400 million deposits two weeks after launch, making Aave the largest DeFi protocol on the
network. Deposits crossed $75 million in the first 24 hours and $300 million by July 14. Announcement here
[https://x.com/aave/status/2078129710916055258].
 
Aave V4 Approaches $300M Deposits
Aave V4 crossed $275 million deposits on July 10, a new all-time high, with caps raised for the seventh time and active loans
approaching $100 million. The first ETH-based incentives on V4 also went live for weETH
[https://x.com/aave/status/2077061561764425875], earned by borrowing ETH against weETH in the EtherFi market.
 




Ecosystem Updates

 * Aave Q2 2026 Livestream Replay [https://x.com/i/broadcasts/1vKpPPXYogVKE]

 * Founder and CEO of Aave Labs, Stani Kulechov, Discusses V4 on Layer One Podcast
   [https://x.com/TheBlockCo/status/2077401158100893734]

 * A New Beginner Series Explains Aave from Zero [https://x.com
